instruction:
Regarding the monotonicity of the function \( y = \frac{x^2}{1 + x} \), which of the following statements is correct?

Options:
A. It is monotonically increasing on \( (-\infty, -2) \)
B. It is monotonically increasing on \( (-1, 0) \)
C. It is monotonically decreasing on \( (-1, 0) \)
D. It is monotonically increasing on \( (-2, -1) \)
E. It is monotonically decreasing on \( (-2, -1) \)
F. It is monotonically decreasing on \( (-\infty, -2) \)
G. It is monotonically decreasing on \( (-\infty, -1) \)
H. It is monotonically increasing on \( (-2, 0) \)
I. It is monotonically increasing on \( (0, +\infty) \)
J. It is monotonically decreasing on \( (-2, 0) \)
